title: Please delete this PR
body: Opened by mistake by an automated agent. Please delete this PR and its branch. Apologies for the noise.

comments.body: The hyperparameter table uses `||` at the start of rows (e.g., `|| Parameter | ...`), which breaks GitHub table rendering. Replace the double leading pipes with a single `|` per row so the table formats correctly.

The STE quantizer clamps to [-128, 127], but the export-time int8 quantization (`quantize_float_tensor`) clamps to [-127, 127]. This mismatch means QAT is training against a different rounding/saturation behavior than what you actually serialize, reducing the likelihood that QAT closes the post-quant gap. Align the STE clamp range (and ideally scale definition) with the export quantizer so training matches the deployed artifact.

The STE quantizer uses per-row `amax` scaling, while the actual post-training quantizer uses percentile clipping (`INT8_CLIP_Q` via `torch.quantile`) before scaling. Since the training simulation doesn't match the export quantization scheme, QAT may optimize for the wrong error model. Consider reusing the same clipping + scaling logic as `quantize_float_tensor` inside the STE (no-grad) path so QAT more directly targets the final int8+zlib roundtrip metric.

`submission.json` has placeholder metrics (`val_loss`, `val_bpb`, `bytes_total`, `bytes_code` all set to 0). Other record submissions in this track include the measured final int8+zlib roundtrip metrics and byte counts; leaving these as 0 makes the record entry misleading and hard to validate. Please populate these fields with the actual run results (and keep the date consistent with the run).

The markdown list under “QAT is applied to:” is written as `|- ...`, which doesn’t render as a proper bullet list in standard Markdown. Use a normal list (blank line followed by `- ...`) so it renders correctly on GitHub.
